articles.title: Wolff on how to 'best handle' Mercedes intra-team battle
articles.summary: Wolff on how to 'best handle' Mercedes intra-team battle. Toto Wolff explains how Mercedes will approach their intra-team battle in 2026 with both Kimi Antonelli and George Russell battling for wins.

articles.title: 12 women who’ve raced and tested F1 cars
articles.summary: 12 women who’ve raced and tested F1 cars. Doriane Pin added her name to the Formula 1 history books last Friday, completing her maiden test at Silverstone, becoming the first woman to drive a Mercedes F1 car – and the first F1 ACADEMY champion to do so.

articles.title: The state of play at Williams
articles.summary: The state of play at Williams. The 2026 season has not gone to plan for Williams – yet. But can the squad turn things around and get back into the midfield fight?

articles.title: Inside Ferrari's fast starts and their impact on betting
articles.summary: Inside Ferrari's fast starts and their impact on betting. Mercedes have won the first three races of the F1 season, but it's Ferrari who have caught the eye of betting fans.

articles.title: Norris wins Laureus World Breakthrough of the Year Award
articles.summary: Norris wins Laureus World Breakthrough of the Year Award. Lando Norris has added another trophy to his growing cabinet after winning the World Breakthrough of the Year prize at the prestigious Laureus World Sports Awards – the 2026 edition being held in Madrid on Monday night.

articles.title: Racing Bulls outline ‘strange’ upgrade plan for next two races
articles.summary: Racing Bulls outline ‘strange’ upgrade plan for next two races. Racing Bulls boss Alan Permane has explained the team's upgrade plans amid the April break in racing.

articles.title: Refinements to 2026 F1 regulations agreed by all stakeholders
articles.summary: Refinements to 2026 F1 regulations agreed by all stakeholders. A number of refinements to the 2026 F1 regulations were agreed during an online meeting between F1 governing body the FIA, Team Principals, CEOs of Power Unit Manufacturers and FOM on Monday.

articles.title: The state of play at Cadillac
articles.summary: The state of play at Cadillac. Cadillac are the new kids on the block, but how has their debut season in F1 started – and what do they need to do to try and bag some points this year?

articles.title: Komatsu highlights 'important quality' Bearman brings to Haas
articles.summary: Komatsu highlights 'important quality' Bearman brings to Haas. Haas Team Principal Ayao Komatsu has explained some of the attributes that make Ollie Bearman such a good team player at Haas.

articles.title: JACQUES: One thing each team needs to work on
articles.summary: JACQUES: One thing each team needs to work on. F1 TV Lead Commentator Alex Jacques assesses one thing each team needs to work on for the Miami Grand Prix, and also into the future.

articles.title: FIA, F1 and teams agree on regulation tweaks ahead of Miami
articles.summary: FIA, F1 and teams agree on regulation tweaks ahead of Miami. Changes to energy deployment and safety have been agreed with the majority set to come into place ahead of the Miami Grand Prix.

articles.summary: F1 plans to ‘act with a scalpel and not a baseball bat’ with rule changes – Wolff. articles.title: Herta’s FP1 debut for Cadillac confirmed in Barcelona
articles.summary: Herta’s FP1 debut for Cadillac confirmed in Barcelona. Colton Herta will make his FP1 debut for Cadillac at the Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix in June, the team has announced.

articles.title: ‘Expectations are automatically a bit different now,’ Antonelli admits
articles.summary: ‘Expectations are automatically a bit different now,’ Antonelli admits. Kimi Antonelli admits the start of the Formula 1 season has gone better than he predicted and has automatically increased expectations as the championship leader.

articles.title: Russell reckons both career achievements and current plight play into Verstappen quit threat
articles.summary: Russell reckons both career achievements and current plight play into Verstappen quit threat. George Russell says he can understand if Max Verstappen takes a break from Formula 1 while he’s not enjoying it, but believes competitive position is playing a significant role in his complaints.

articles.title: Verstappen says he told Lambiase he’d be ‘stupid’ not to take McLaren offer
articles.summary: Verstappen says he told Lambiase he’d be ‘stupid’ not to take McLaren offer. Max Verstappen told his race engineer Gianpiero Lambiase he would be stupid not to take the offer he received from McLaren, after asking the Dutchman for his thoughts on the move.

articles.title: Norris believes F1 is not too far from ‘a really exciting era’
articles.summary: Norris believes F1 is not too far from ‘a really exciting era’. Lando Norris believes Formula 1 is close to “having a really exciting era of racing” if the sport can put more control in the driver’s hands with tweaks to the 2026 regulations.

articles.title: McLaren drivers still sure their MCL40 is a winner
articles.summary: McLaren drivers still sure their MCL40 is a winner. Oscar Piastri and Lando Norris both believe McLaren has the potential to be a consistent race-winning team this season, despite Mercedes’ early dominance.

articles.title: Herta drawing strength from paddock welcome as he grapples with a new racing paradigm in F2
articles.summary: Herta drawing strength from paddock welcome as he grapples with a new racing paradigm in F2. While acknowledging the high level of challenge having made the switch from IndyCar to Formula 2,  Colton Herta says that he feels welcomed into the Formula 1 ladder.

articles.summary: Red Bull's difficult start and 1976 cars compared with 2026 - F1 Q&A. BBC Sport F1 correspondent Andrew Benson answers your latest questions.

articles.title: F1 makes changes to address new engine rules concerns
articles.summary: F1 makes changes to address new engine rules concerns. Formula 1 makes a series of rule changes to address concerns about the new engine regulations that were introduced for this season.

articles.title: F1 bosses to use 'scalpel rather than baseball bat'
articles.summary: F1 bosses to use 'scalpel rather than baseball bat'. F1 bosses will use "a scalpel rather than a baseball bat" in making changes to the sport's rules at a meeting on Monday, says Toto Wolff.

articles.summary: Miettinen dies in seven-car crash during Nurburgring race. Racing driver Juha Miettinen dies following a seven-car crash during the qualifiers for a race in Germany.

articles.summary: Britain's youngest F1 driver on his debut season so far - and learning to skateboard. The youngest ever British F1 driver is looks forward to a lifelong dream of racing at Silverstone.

articles.title: Russell 'would understand' if Verstappen leaves F1
articles.summary: Russell 'would understand' if Verstappen leaves F1. George Russell says Formula 1 "would not want to lose" Max Verstappen - but would "understand" if the four-time world champion decided to leave the sport.

articles.summary: What it's really like to try to make it to F1. BBC Sport explores the financial reality of two drivers with experience of trying to climb the motorsport ladder to reach Formula 1.

articles.title: Maybe Verstappen needs a break from F1? - Hill
articles.summary: Maybe Verstappen needs a break from F1? - Hill. On the latest episode of The Chequered Flag Podcast, 1996 F1 world champion Damon Hill thinks that if Max Verstappen is not enjoying the sport any more following regulation changes then perhaps he needs to take a break.

articles.title: Antonelli celebrations 'lovely to see' - Benson
articles.summary: Antonelli celebrations 'lovely to see' - Benson. On the latest episode of the The Chequered Flag Podcast, pundits Andrew Benson and Sam Bird say Kimi Antonelli's Chinese Grand Prix win at 19 years old "is as big as it comes" for a young driver, after the Italian was moved to tears following his win.
